💧

Citizen Science

Water Testing

Since 2018, FGVW volunteers have been monitoring the water quality of Gledhow Beck — the stream that runs through the heart of the valley — as part of our citizen science programme.

Why water quality matters

Gledhow Beck is an urban watercourse — it flows through a catchment of residential streets, roads, parks, and gardens before passing through the ancient woodland of Gledhow Valley. Like many urban becks in Leeds, it is vulnerable to pollution from road runoff, misconnected drains, and diffuse sources in the surrounding area.

Good water quality in Gledhow Beck is vital for the health of the whole woodland ecosystem. The beck supports invertebrates that are the basis of the food chain for fish, birds such as the kingfisher, and amphibians. Pollution in the water affects all of these species.

How we test

Our trained volunteers carry out monthly water quality testing at fixed sampling points along Gledhow Beck. We measure a range of parameters including:

  • Phosphate — an indicator of sewage and agricultural pollution
  • Nitrate — from fertilisers and organic matter
  • pH — acidity/alkalinity of the water
  • Biological oxygen demand (BOD) — a measure of organic pollution
  • Turbidity — water clarity
  • Invertebrate sampling — used as a biological indicator of water quality

Results are recorded and reported to Leeds City Council and to our partners. Significant findings are reported to the Environment Agency.

Water quality documentary

A documentary film about urban water quality monitoring in Leeds — including the work being done by groups like FGVW — has been produced in association with Friends of Roundhay Park.

Visit FoRP water monitoring project →

Related organisations

  • Friends of Roundhay Park (FoRP) →

    Water monitoring project for Roundhay Park's lakes and watercourses.

  • REAP →

    Responsible, Ethical and Accountable Procurement — a partner organisation in water quality monitoring.

Image: FGVW volunteer taking a water sample from Gledhow Beck — photography placeholder

Latest Reports

1 December 2025

Gledhow Beck Water Quality Report 2025

Annual summary of water quality monitoring results for Gledhow Beck, including phosphate, nitrate, pH, and biological oxygen demand measurements taken throughout 2025.

View report →

Get involved in water monitoring

Our water monitoring volunteers are trained by experienced practitioners. If you're interested in getting involved in citizen science and learning how to assess water quality, we'd love to hear from you.

Get in touch

Friends of Gledhow Valley Woods is a registered charity protecting and enhancing Gledhow Valley Woods in north Leeds since 1996.

Registered Charity No. 1169599
